address to {}\\\", listener_name,\\n126:             local_address_->asString());\\n127: \\n128:   // Now create the remainder of the sockets that will be used by the rest of the workers.\\n129:   for }\\n140: \\n141: ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\\n142:     : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\\n155:     //\\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\\n162:   }\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. }\\n140: \\n141: ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\\n142:     : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), local_address_(factory_to_clone.local_address_),\\n143:       socket_type_(factory_to_clone.socket_type_), options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\\n144:       listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\\n155:     //\\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\\n162:   }\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. Make that code not return nullptr\\n169:   // so this code can be simpler.\\n170:   absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> socket_or_error = factory.createListenSocket(\\n171:       local_address_, socket_type, options_, bind_type_, socket_creation_options_, worker_index);\\n172:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(socket_or_error.status());\\n173:   Network::SocketSharedPtr socket = std::move(*socket_or_error);\\n174: \\n175:   // Binding is done by now.\\n176:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"Create listen socket for listener {} on address {}\\\", listener_name_,\\n177:             local_address_->asString());\\n178:   if (socket != nullptr && options_ != nullptr) {\\n179:     const bool ok = Network::Socket::applyOptions(\\n180:         options_, *socket, envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption::STATE_BOUND);\\n181:     const std::string message =\\n182:         fmt::format(\\\"{}: Setting socket options {}\\\", listener_name_, ok ? \\\"succeeded\\\" : \\\"failed\\\");\\n183:     if (!ok) {\\n184:       ENVOY_LOG(warn, \\\"{}\\\", message);\\n185:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\\n186:     } else {\\n187:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"{}\\\", message);\\n188:     }\\n189: \\n190:     // Add the options to the socket_ so that STATE_LISTENING options can be\\n191:     // set after listen() is called and immediately before the workers start running.\\n192:     socket->addOptions(options_);\\n193:   }\\n194:   return socket;\\n195: }\\n196: \\n197: Network::SocketSharedPtr ListenSocketFactoryImpl::getListenSocket(uint32_t worker_index) {\\n198:   // Per the TODO above, sockets at this point can never be null. options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\n144:       listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\n155:     //\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. \n512: absl::Status\n513: ListenerManagerImpl::setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n514:                                                    const ListenerImpl& existing_listener) }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713: \n813: void ListenerManagerImpl::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use Specifically on\\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\\n152:     // on the same address. }\\n140: \\n141: ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\\n142:     : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\\n155:     //\\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\\n162:   }\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\n144:       listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\n145:       tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\n146:       bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\n147:       socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\n148:   for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\n149:     // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\n150:     // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\n151:     // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\n152:     // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\n153:     // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\n154:     // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\n155:     //\n156:     // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\n157:     // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\n158:     // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\n159:     // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\n160:     // probably not worth the difficulty.)\n161:     sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\n162:   }\n163: }\n164: \n165: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\n166:     ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\n167:   // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\n168:   // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. ListenerImpl::diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\n1069:                                    std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback) {\n1070:   for (const auto& ListenerImpl::getReusePortOrDefault(Server::Instance& server,\n1090:                                          const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n1091:                                          Network::Socket::Type socket_type) {\n1092:   bool initial_reuse_port_value = [&server, &config]() {\n1093:     // If someone set the new field, adhere to it.\n1094:     if preferring \"\n1098:                   \"enable_reuse_port.\",\n1099:                   config.name());\n1100:       }\n1101: \n1102:       return config.enable_reuse_port().value();\n1103:     }\n1104: \n1105:     // If someone set the old field to true, adhere to it.\n1106:     if (config.reuse_port()) {\n1107:       return true;\n1108:     }\n1109: \n1110:     // Otherwise use the server default which depends on hot restart.\n1111:     return server.enableReusePortDefault();\n1112:   }();\n1113: \n1114: #ifndef __linux__\n1115:   if (initial_reuse_port_value && socket_type == Multiple zero\n1141:   // port addresses are also supported. For comparing two listeners with multiple\n1142:   // zero port addresses, only need to ensure there are the same number of zero\n1143:   // port addresses. So create a copy of other's addresses here, remove it for any\n1144:   // finding to avoid matching same zero port address to the same one.\n1145:   auto other_addresses = other.addresses();\n1146:   for (auto& addr : addresses()) {\n1147:     auto iter = std::find_if(other_addresses.begin(), other_addresses.end(),\n1148:                              [&addr](const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& other_addr) {\n1149:                                return *addr == *other_addr;\n1150:                              });\n1151:     if (iter == other_addresses.end()) {\n1152:       return false;\n1153:     }\n1154:     other_addresses.erase(iter);\n1155:   }\n1156:   return true;\n1157: }\n1158: \n1159: bool ListenerImpl::hasDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const {\n1160:   // Skip the duplicate address check if this is the case of a listener update with new socket\n1161:   // options.\n1162:   if ((name_ == other.name_) &&\n1163:       !ListenerMessageUtil::socketOptionsEqual(config(), other.config())) {\n1164:     return false;\n1165:   }\n1166: \n1167:   if (socket_type_ != other.socket_type_) {\n1168:     return false;\n1169:   }\n1170:   // For listeners that do not bind or listeners that do not bind to port 0 we must check to make\n1171:   // sure we are not duplicating the address. ListenerOrchestrator::addOrUpdateListenerInternal(\n538:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n539:     bool added_via_api, const std::string& active listener\");\n614:       *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n615:     }\n616:   } else {\n617:     // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n623:     } else {\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add active listener\");\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n626:     }\n627: \n628:     added = true;\n629:   }\n630: \n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n632:   if (added) {\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\n634:   } else {\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n636:   }\n637: \n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\n639:   return true;\n640: }\n641: \n642: bool ListenerOrchestrator::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\n646:       return true;\n647:     }\n648:   }\n649:   return false;\n650: }\n651: \n652: void ListenerOrchestrator::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\n656: \n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n658:   // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n660: \n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n668:       }\n669:     }\n670:   });\n671: \n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n678:       // to remove the listener.\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n688:           }\n689:         });\n690:       });\n691:     }\n692:   });\n693: \n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n695: }\n696: \n697: ListenerOrchestrator::ListenerList::iterator\n698: ListenerOrchestrator::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\n703:       // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\n704:       ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\n705:       ret = it;\n706:     }\n707:   }\n708:   return ret;\n709: }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerOrchestrator::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n714: \n715:   size_t size = 0;\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\n720: \n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n729:     }\n730:   }\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n734:     }\n735:   }\n736:   return ret;\n737: }\n738: \n739: bool ListenerOrchestrator::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n740: \n813: void ListenerOrchestrator::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969: 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: void ListenerOrchestrator::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n1006:   for (const auto& filter_chain_factory_context) const {\n1074:   // If the cluster doesn't have transport socket configured, then use the default \"raw_buffer\"\n1075:   // transport socket or BoringSSL-based \"tls\" \"envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager\";\n1102:   if (is_quic &&\n1103:       (filter_chain.filters().empty() ||\n1104: compiled out it should not be possible to configure either the QUIC transport\n1112:   // socket or the QUIC listener and get to this point.\n1113:   ASSERT(!is_quic);\n1114: #endif\n1115:   ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1140:   if (hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(warming_listeners_, listener) ||\n1141:       hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(active_listeners_, listener)) {\n1142:
